rulings.Case Title: LATAYA ROBINSON VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend To Include Punitive Damages Allegations
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 3, 2025, Line 1, PLAINTIFF LATAYA ROBINSON Notice Of Motion To Amend To Include Punitive Damages Allegations Plaintiff Lataya Robinson's motion to Amend To Include Punitive Damages Allegations is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RENEWAL IN THE EVENT OF A SUFFICIENT TRIAL CONTINUANCE. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) To add a request for punitive damages, Robinson seeks leave to file a second amended complaint pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 576, which states that "[a]ny judge, at any time before or after commencement of trial, in the furtherance of justice, and upon such terms as may be proper, may allow the amendment of any pleading or pretrial conference order." Because judicial policy favors resolution of all disputed matters between the parties in the same lawsuit, the court's discretion will usually be exercised liberally to permit amendment of the pleadings. (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 6:638 (Rutter 2023); see also Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6 Cal.3d 920, 939.) Indeed, "it is an abuse of discretion to deny leave to amend where the opposing party was not misled or prejudiced by the amendment." (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Superior Court (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER WATKINS VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., 1515 3RD ST SAN FRANCISCO, CA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Judgement On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 3, 2025, Line 2, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. Motion And Notice Of Judgement On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Uber's unopposed requests for judicial notice of Exhibits 1-3 and 6-17 are granted. (See Evid. Code 452(d).) Judicial notice of Exhibits 4 and 5 is denied. On its own motion, the court strikes Watkins' unauthorized sur-reply. Watkins sued Uber in April 2023, alleging he was improperly compensated and assaulted by passengers while driving for Uber. The court sustained Uber's unopposed demurrer to the original complaint with leave to amend in June 2024. Watkins filed his first amended complaint (FAC) in August 2024, reasserting his negligence claims for improper compensation and exposure to emotionally abusive passengers. The FAC added allegations of negligence related to delays in processing Watkins's background check, and "emotional distress," which seems to involve an intentional infliction of emotional distress claim. Uber now moves for judgment on the pleadings to the entire FAC on grounds that it fails to state a claim. (CCP 430.10(e).) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: 2075 SUTTER STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. EAST BAY CONSTRUCTION & MANAGEMENT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Terminating Sanctions Against East Bay Construction And Management Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 3, 2025, Line 3, PLAINTIFF 2075 SUTTER STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION Motion For Order Terminating Sanctions Against East Bay Construction And Management LLC Plaintiff 2075 Sutter Street Homeowners Association's Motion For Order Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant East Bay Construction And Management LLC is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) Defendant failed to timely respond to Plaintiff's form interrogatories and request for production of documents set one. The court granted Plaintiff's motion to compel responses and ordered Defendant to pay sanctions of $750 to Plaintiff. Defendant filed a motion to vacate, which failed, then a motion for reconsideration, which failed. After this motion was filed, Defendant provided responses to the discovery and paid the sanctions. Plaintiff contends the responses are inadequate. In the meantime, Defendant served a substitution of counsel, purporting to substitute a non-attorney representative as "counsel," which, of course, is improper and unlawful. Defendant represents this was a mistake and has filed a substitution with new counsel. Plaintiff moves for an order striking Defendant's answer and entering default against Defendant. (See CCP Section Code 2023.030(d).) Defendant opposes. In the reply, Plaintiff states Defendant is not complying with expert discovery demands. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCESS ABROGAR VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 3, 2025, Line 4, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, OFFICER LARS NELSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Motion For Summary Adjudication Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Lars Nelson's motion for summary judgment, or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) On March 31, 2023, Plaintiff Princess Abrogar was pulled over while driving, detained, and arrested. Abrogar alleges the arresting officers used unlawful force during the arrest. She sued the City and various officers for negligence, battery, and violation of the Bane Act (Civil Code 52.1). The City and arresting officer Nelson now move for summary judgment, or alternatively, summary adjudication. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
